reasoning: The three key sprites are placed against a dark navy background and carry warm gold/white pixel-art coloring that provides adequate luminance contrast. Stylistically they match the game's ornate pixel aesthetic (gold filigree, similar palette to dungeon UI buttons), so they feel native rather than pasted. However, all three items are rendered at a small scale and their silhouettes are tall, thin vertical shapes — indistinct from generic staff or wand props at a quick glance. Without the red arrow annotation, a player would likely need more than 0.5 s to consciously identify them as 'boss keys'. The dark background is actually helpful here (no competing tile hues), and the blue accent on one key adds a second differentiating color read. No serious tonal clash with the Echoes aesthetic. Main weakness is scale and silhouette clarity, not color integration.
